affordances.name: Feedback threshold local optimization check
affordances.mechanism: When small moves may trigger delayed blowback, threshold crossings, or whole-system degradation, require the answer to identify feedback loops, timing effects, and the concrete lever/checkpoint that keeps complexity from becoming an excuse.

use_when: Modest interventions may trigger disproportionate upside, delayed blowback, or threshold crossings.
Reinforcing or balancing loops, compounding interactions, or timing effects matter more than one-step cause-and-effect logic.
Fixing one metric, team, or process in isolation may degrade long-run system behavior elsewhere.
do_not_use_when: Complexity language is being used to avoid concrete levers, checkpoints, or experiments.
The problem is linear enough that local optimization has no meaningful system spillover.
The answer would explain complexity but leave no monitorable action or stop/change threshold.
case_evidence_needed: The local intervention or optimization being proposed.
The feedback loop, delay, or threshold that could change system behavior.
The metric or area that could degrade elsewhere.
The lever, checkpoint, or experiment that remains actionable under uncertainty.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the feedback loop, delay, or threshold that makes the system non-linear, then state the concrete lever, checkpoint, or experiment that can still improve the system.
evidence_required: The feedback loop or timing effect.
good_output_shape: Names the delayed or disproportionate effect.
Protects against local optimization harming the whole.
Ends with a concrete lever, checkpoint, or bounded experiment.

diagnostic_questions: What feedback loop or delay could make this local move behave non-linearly?
Which whole-system metric could worsen while the local metric improves?
What concrete checkpoint prevents complexity talk from replacing operational choice?
misuse_guards: Do not invoke complexity to avoid choosing a lever.
Do not optimize a local metric without checking whole-system behavior.
Do not leave nonlinear analysis without a bounded experiment or monitoring threshold.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when small moves can create outsized effects
Works when feedback loops dominate the outcome
Best when local optimization can hurt the whole system

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 9 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports one nonlinear feedback affordance focused on loops, delays, thresholds, and local optimization side effects.
dropped_material: Did not extract organizational design or AI persona simulation as separate affordances.
Kept systems maps and simulations as tools under the same operational card.
open_questions: PR43 should test whether this card makes nonlinear risk more actionable rather than more abstract.

absence_records.reason: The source rejects invoking complexity to avoid concrete levers, checkpoints, or experiments that could improve the system under uncertainty.

source_evidence.source_quote: Danger When Complexity Talk Excuses Lack of Operational Choice

absence_records.reason: The source says outcomes in complex systems rarely follow a straight-line progression, so PR42 must not turn this into a simple local fix card.

source_evidence.source_quote: outcomes in complex systems rarely follow a straight-line progression

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 9 source reading found strong reviewed depth for non-linear-dynamics as feedback-loop and threshold discipline with an explicit operational-choice guard.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card helps the next LLM catch local optimization harm.
